item.title: Convicted Utah author's son reveals new details about night his dad died
item.description: Prosecutors said Kouri Richins laced her husband's cocktail with five times the lethal dose of fentanyl in 2022.

item.title: Trump promised to hold 30,000 migrants at Guantanamo. It's mostly empty.
item.description: A CBS News review of internal government documents and information provided to Congress shows immigration detention facilities at Guantanamo Bay are nearly empty.

item.title: Iran says it's ready to "teach a lesson" if U.S. launches new attacks
item.description: Iran warns any new U.S. attacks will bring a "bad result," as President Trump acknowledges the ceasefire is faltering and violence flares in Lebanon.

item.title: Trump heading to China for high-stakes meeting with Xi
item.description: Trade, Taiwan and tensions with Iran are surefire topics for President Trump's meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

item.title: IDPH investigating possible hantavirus in Winnebago Co. not linked to cruise
item.description: The Illinois Department of Public Health said it is investigating a potential case of hantavirus in an Illinois resident, that they said is not linked to the deadly cruise ship outbreak.

item.title: Watch: Pete Hegseth faces lawmakers' questions as he vies for $1.5 trillion defense budget
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Gen. Dan Caine faced bipartisan questioning on Capitol Hill about the war in Iran on Tuesday. The Trump administration is seeking $1.5 trillion for defense in the 2027 budget proposal, a starting point for negotiations. Hegseth called it "admittedly a historic budget" in his opening statement.

item.title: What to know about the primaries in Nebraska and West Virginia
item.description: Voters went to the polls in Nebraska and West Virginia on Tuesday, with Democrats vying for the chance to run in an open seat in Nebraska that the party has long been eyeing.

item.title: Florida's "Alligator Alcatraz" could close in June, sources say
item.description: The closure comes amid escalating operating costs for the facility, which are now estimated to total nearly $1 billion.

item.title: Highlights from previous Trump-Xi meetings
item.description: Wednesday will mark the seventh meeting between President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping. Watch CBS News' coverage of previous meetings between the world leaders through the years.

item.title: 5/12: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: President Trump heads to Bejing to meet with Xi Jinping; inflation surges to its highest level in nearly three years.

item.title: NAACP president calls Supreme Court redistricting decision "one of the worst" in history
item.description: Republican Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey is pushing back primaries for four Congressional districts following Monday's Supreme Court decision allowing the state to use a 2023 map with only one majority-Black district. Other states are also looking to redraw their maps after the decision. NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Why health officials expect hantavirus cases to continue rising
item.description: Global health officials are warning that hantavirus cases stemming from a cruise ship outbreak could continue to rise. Dr. Abraar Karan, with Stanford University's division of infectious diseases, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Harvard voting on limiting "A" grades for undergrad students to curb grade inflation
item.description: Harvard University faculty members are voting on potentially limiting the number of "A's" professors can give students in an attempt to curb rising grade inflation. Axios Boston reporter Mike Deehan joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47 from brain cancer
item.description: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died, his family announced Tuesday.

item.title: How an elementary school custodian earned the title of "Papa Duck"
item.description: Outside of Chicago, at Western Avenue Elementary, the head custodian has been looking out for little ducklings for 29 years. Matt Gutman has the story.

item.title: New details about Boston-area highway gunman
item.description: The suspect who allegedly fired into a major road near Boston had prior criminal convictions. Jericka Duncan reports on new details about the shooting and the suspect.

item.title: Will Trump seek investment to join China in a "dark factory" future?
item.description: A Chinese manufacturing giant tells CBS News how its sprawling factory runs with a fraction of the human workforce previously required.

item.title: Florida immigration detention center "Alligator Alcatraz" to close, sources say
item.description: The immigration detention center in the Florida Everglades known as "Alligator Alcatraz" is set to close less than a year after it opened, sources told CBS News.

item.title: Plane crashes in Atlantic Ocean, all 11 passengers rescued
item.description: A small plane crashed into the Atlantic Ocean on Tuesday, 50 miles off Vero Beach, Florida, with 11 people on board. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Economic pressure may not push Iranian regime to make peace deal with U.S., expert explains
item.description: President Trump is on his way to Beijing to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Before he left, he spoke about the current ceasefire with Iran. Will Todman, a senior fellow in the Middle East Program at CSIS, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Democrats grill Kash Patel on accusations of excessive drinking and other personal behavior
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defended the Pentagon's budget request of $1.5 trillion on Tuesday. At a separate hearing, FBI Director Kash Patel faced questions about his alcohol use and personal behavior.

item.title: What's Trump's game plan in China amid Iran war?
item.description: President Trump headed to China on Tuesday for a high-stakes and highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Before leaving, Mr. Trump downplayed the need for Xi to intervene in the stalled peace talks with Iran. Anna Coren reports from Beijing.

item.title: Trump on "the only thing that matters" in dealing with Iran
item.description: President Trump said Americans' financial situation isn't motivating him to make a deal, "Not even a little bit," and that he is only focused on preventing Iran from getting nuclear weapons.

item.title: CPI surged in April as inflation soars to highest level in almost 3 years
item.description: U.S. consumer prices rose in April, fueled by a spike in energy prices caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Local restaurant owner feels the pinch of war with Iran
item.description: Jason Allen has a closer look at how rising prices are impacting both American consumers and business owners in Dallas.

item.title: Trump administration to name David Venturella as interim ICE chief
item.description: The Trump administration plans to name longtime immigration official David Venturella as the interim head of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, replacing acting director Todd Lyons, a spokesperson and two U.S. officials said.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: LBJ calls for equal voting rights after Selma march in 1965
item.description: Major Garrett introduces CBS News Radio coverage of President Lyndon B. Johnson's 1965 speech to Congress, calling for equal voting rights for Black Americans.

item.title: 5/12: CBS Evening News
item.description: President Trump says he's not backing down as Iran war spikes cost of living; California mayor pleads guilty to acting as an illegal agent for China.

item.title: Social Security's cost-of-living adjustment could reach nearly 4% in 2027
item.description: A larger COLA would boost monthly checks for retirees, but also strain Social Security's already depleted trust funds.

item.title: Ukraine and U.S. move toward landmark drone defense deal
item.description: A deal is taking shape for the U.S. and Ukraine to jointly develop and build weapons that have been at the forefront of the wars in both Ukraine and Iran.
